prompt: Scrape https://example.com and return markdown.
rationale: Single URL fetch/extract request with an explicit output format belongs to web_scrape.

prompt: Read https://example.com as markdown.
rationale: Natural single URL read request with markdown output belongs to web_scrape.

prompt: List the URLs available from https://example.com.
rationale: Listing URLs available from a site is URL inventory/discovery, so use web_map instead of reading pages.

prompt: Crawl https://example.com, up to 25 pages.
rationale: The user asks to crawl and read linked pages with a page limit, so use web_crawl.

prompt: Compare https://example.com against my homepage snapshot.
rationale: Comparing a URL against a named homepage snapshot is web_diff.

prompt: Crawl this docs site to depth 2 and collect linked pages.
rationale: The user asks to crawl linked pages and read page bodies, not just discover URL inventories.

prompt: Find URLs from robots.txt, sitemaps, and llms.txt without reading page content.
rationale: Discovery-only robots/sitemaps/llms inventory maps URLs and does not fetch page content.

prompt: Fetch these 5 independent URLs and report per-URL failures.
rationale: Multiple independent URLs with per-URL success/failure semantics belong to web_batch.

prompt: Retrieve the job manifest for jobId crawl-abc123.
rationale: Retrieving a stored response or structured job manifest by jobId belongs to web_get_result.

prompt: Compare the homepage against the saved homepage snapshot.
rationale: Comparing current content with a named saved snapshot is web_diff.

prompt: List supported deterministic extractors and URL patterns.
rationale: Listing available deterministic known-site extractors is web_extract action=list, not page extraction.

prompt: Inspect this raw README URL for deterministic markers: print length, positions for ## Tools and scrape, and regex matches for web_[a-z_]+ without writing a Python script: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/0xMassi/webclaw/main/README.md
rationale: Marker positions, contains checks, bounded excerpts, and regex matches are deterministic text inspection, so use web_extract action=pattern instead of shell/Python or model-backed adhoc extraction.

prompt: Get typed npm metadata for https://www.npmjs.com/package/pi-scraper.
rationale: A known npm package URL should use deterministic typed extraction through web_extract action=vertical instead of ad hoc LLM JSON.

prompt: Get typed DeepWiki metadata for https://deepwiki.com/owner/repo.
rationale: A DeepWiki URL is a supported known-site deterministic extractor case under web_extract action=vertical.

prompt: Extract the structured sections and API signature from this MDN/Docusaurus docs page as deterministic JSON: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API
rationale: Documentation platform pages with sections/API reference layout are deterministic docs-site vertical extraction, so use web_extract action=vertical extractor=docsite instead of flat scrape or ad hoc model JSON.

prompt: Extract structured metadata and top comments from this Reddit post: https://www.reddit.com/r/announcements/comments/14f4h6s/reddit_api_changes_subreddit_blackouts_and_how/
rationale: Known-site structured extraction for a public Reddit post belongs to the reddit vertical extractor, not generic scraping or broad search.

prompt: Search this Jupyter notebook JSON for code cells mentioning 'tool calls' using JSONPath: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/openai-cookbook/main/examples/How_to_count_tokens_with_tiktoken.ipynb
rationale: Deterministic JSONPath field selection and excerpt search over a notebook or API JSON payload belongs to web_extract action=pattern with sourceFormat=json and jsonPaths, replacing one-off Python scripts.

prompt: From this arbitrary product page, extract name and price as JSON.
rationale: Arbitrary page JSON/schema extraction is LLM-backed web_extract action=adhoc when no deterministic vertical applies.

prompt: Summarize this single page in five bullets.
rationale: A one-page or provided-content summary uses web_summarize, not multi-source research.

prompt: Check crawl status for crawlId docs-123.
rationale: Prior crawl status metadata is now handled by web_crawl action=status.

prompt: List prior crawls for this docs seed.
rationale: Listing prior local crawls by seed/status belongs to web_crawl action=list.

prompt: Resume crawl docs-123.
rationale: Resuming stored crawl work is a web_crawl action=run request with crawlId and resume.

prompt: Summarize https://example.com.
rationale: One concrete URL with a page-scoped summary request should use web_summarize, not multi-source research.

prompt: Extract JSON from GitHub repo URL https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript.
rationale: Known GitHub repository URL should prefer deterministic web_extract action=vertical over ad hoc extraction.

prompt: List pages on this site from sitemap files only, without reading page bodies.
rationale: Discovery-only listing from sitemap files should map URLs and avoid page-content fetches.

prompt: List pages on this site by following links and reading pages to depth 1.
rationale: Following links and reading pages is a crawl run, not discovery-only web_map.

prompt: Research the best web scrapers across multiple sources.
rationale: Multi-source research should use a companion search/research extension, not pi-scraper scraping tools.

prompt: Find recent articles about AI agents.
rationale: Open-ended discovery/search without a concrete URL belongs to search/research, not web_map.

prompt: Summarize these five sources into a report with citations.
rationale: Multi-source synthesis with citations is research/reporting, not page-scoped web_summarize.

prompt: Monitor the web for future changes.
rationale: Future monitoring is not a single scrape/diff action unless the user provides a URL and snapshot target.

prompt: Translate this paragraph.
rationale: Translation is unrelated to scraping, extraction, crawling, mapping, diffing, or page summaries.

prompt: Extract the site logo, colors, fonts, favicons, and Open Graph assets.
rationale: The request does not provide a URL or content source; do not call a scraper or extractor without an input page.

prompt: Search stored scrape text for pricing table.
rationale: Stored local full-text search is no longer public and is not broad web search.
